BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Above approximately 1800 m the wind slabs are larger. Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in particular in places that are protected from the wind. In all aspects medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: A lot of snow will fall. In some places new snow and wind slabs are lying on a moist old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Since Monday the wind has been strong.

The clearly visible wind slabs of the last two days can be released by a single winter sport participant in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers and reach large size.

Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.

In the afternoon the likelihood of dry and moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of a strong northeasterly wind, sometimes deep wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.
In addition soft wind slabs formed also in places that are protected from the wind. They are quite prevalent but are difficult to recognise.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. Low and intermediate altitudes as well as steep sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust will soften during the day.

Adjacent to ridgelines in high Alpine regions snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack: Various wind slab layers are lying on a hard crust.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Thursday: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The wind was strong. As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs formed also in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
The wind slabs can be released, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm sign. The wind slabs are barely recognisable.
Especially here the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will gradually increase, in particular on very steep sunny slopes. As the day progresses, moist snow slides are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the past few hours above approximately 2000 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong.
In some localities 5 to 20 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As you gain altitude, snow cover decreases and becomes uneven. Snow cover is often better at intermediate altitudes, where it's less affected by the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, in particular in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the snow drift accumulations. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northerly wind, extensive wind slabs formed in the last few days in all aspects. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes dry slab avalanches are possible. Avalanches can be released by small loads. This applies in particular above the tree line, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in east to north to west facing aspects. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. These can release deeper layers of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on east to north to west facing aspects and generally above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Thursday: The avalanche danger will decrease.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed in all aspects. As a consequence of a sometimes strong northeasterly wind, further wind slabs will form on Wednesday. These can be released easily above the tree line. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation loose snow avalanches are to be expected, but they will be mostly small. This applies in particular on extremely steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in all aspects. The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the wind slabs have increased in size moderately. These can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are barely recognisable.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell in the past few hours above approximately 2000 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong.
In some localities 2 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As you gain altitude, snow cover decreases and becomes uneven. Snow cover is often better at intermediate altitudes, where it's less affected by the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, in particular in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the snow drift accumulations. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can still be released. This applies in particular in case of a large load. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The surface of the snowpack is generally characterised by alternating areas of erosion interspersed with areas of accumulation; in many areas there are hard surface crusts, some of which are hidden. Near the ridges, the wind over the past few days has led to the formation of snow overhanging mass. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be partly cloudy. The wind will be moderate.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a storm force northerly wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed in all aspects. As a consequence of a sometimes strong northeasterly wind, further wind slabs will form on Wednesday. These can be released even by a single winter sport participant above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow has fallen in some regions. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the fresh and old snow. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1400 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The wind slabs are easy to recognise but to be assessed critically.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northeasterly directions, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. These can be released by a single winter sport participant.

On very steep slopes the avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Since Monday the wind has been strong over a wide area. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Especially slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the top section of the snowpack.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

The snowpack will be well bonded at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The more recent wind slabs represent the main danger. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Thursday: The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The wind will be moderate to strong. The wind has transported some snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow has fallen since Sunday in some regions. Since Monday the wind has been strong. The clearly visible wind slabs of the last two days can be released by a single winter sport participant in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.

In the afternoon the likelihood of dry and moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions 5 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1700 m.
As a consequence of a strong northwesterly wind, hard wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. Low and intermediate altitudes as well as steep sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust will soften during the day.

Adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Over a wide area various wind slab layers are lying on a hard crust.

Below the wind-eroded peaks, above around 1600 m, more snow is lying for the time of year.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Thursday: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In particular on steep slopes small avalanches are possible as a consequence of the snowfall. As a consequence of the snowfall the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Some snow will fall. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a moist old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be generally stable. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
